title: Litomyšl

infobox.value: (Czech pronunciation: [[ˈlɪtomɪʃl̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Czech "Help:IPA/Czech") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Cs_Litomy%C5%A1l.ogg "File:Cs Litomyšl.ogg"); [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Leitomischl*) is a town in [Svitavy District](/wiki/Svitavy_District "Svitavy District") in the [Pardubice Region](/wiki/Pardubice_Region "Pardubice Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 10,000 inhabitants. It is a former bishopric and Latin Catholic [titular see](/wiki/Titular_see "Titular see"). Litomyšl is known for the [château](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau "Château")-type castle complex of the [Litomyšl Castle](/wiki/Litomy%C5%A1l_Castle "Litomyšl Castle"), which is a [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") [World Heritage Site](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site"). The historic town centre with the castle complex is well preserved and is protected as an [urban monument reservation](/wiki/Cultural_monument_(Czech_Republic)#Monument_reservations "Cultural monument (Czech Republic)").

**Litomyšl** (Czech pronunciation: [[ˈlɪtomɪʃl̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Czech "Help:IPA/Czech") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Cs_Litomy%C5%A1l.ogg "File:Cs Litomyšl.ogg"); [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Leitomischl*) is a town in [Svitavy District](/wiki/Svitavy_District "Svitavy District") in the [Pardubice Region](/wiki/Pardubice_Region "Pardubice Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 10,000 inhabitants. It is a former bishopric and Latin Catholic [titular see](/wiki/Titular_see "Titular see"). Litomyšl is known for the [château](/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau "Château")-type castle complex of the [Litomyšl Castle](/wiki/Litomy%C5%A1l_Castle "Litomyšl Castle"), which is a [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") [World Heritage Site](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site"). The historic town centre with the castle complex is well preserved and is protected as an [urban monument reservation](/wiki/Cultural_monument_(Czech_Republic)#Monument_reservations "Cultural monument (Czech Republic)").

## Administrative division

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Administrative division")]

Litomyšl consists of ten municipal parts (in brackets population according to the 2021 census):[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* Litomyšl-město (6,026)
* [Kornice](/wiki/Kornice "Kornice") (137)
* Lány (441)
* Nedošín (526)
* Nová Ves u Litomyšle (99)
* Pazucha (130)
* Pohodlí (290)
* Suchá (101)
* Zahájí (1,147)
* Záhradí (1,130)

Nová Ves u Litomyšle and Pohodlí form an [exclave](/wiki/Enclave_and_exclave "Enclave and exclave") of the municipal territory.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The name is derived from the personal name Litomysl (in old Czech written as Ľutomysl), meaning "Litomysl's (castle)".[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography")]

Litomyšl is located about 16 kilometres (10 mi) northwest of [Svitavy](/wiki/Svitavy "Svitavy") and 41 km (25 mi) southeast of [Pardubice](/wiki/Pardubice "Pardubice"). It lies in the [Svitavy Uplands](/wiki/Svitavy_Uplands "Svitavy Uplands"). The highest point is at 558 m (1,831 ft) above sea level. The [Loučná](/wiki/Lou%C4%8Dn%C3%A1_(river) "Loučná (river)") River flows through the town. There are several fishponds in the municipal territory; the largest of them is Velký Košíř, located northwest of the town proper.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f2/Litomysl_zamek.jpg/250px-Litomysl_zamek.jpg)](/wiki/File:Litomysl_zamek.jpg)

Litomyšl Castle

The first written mention of Litomyšl is from 981. It is a record in *[Chronica Boemorum](/wiki/Chronica_Boemorum "Chronica Boemorum")* mentioning death of Duke [Slavník](/wiki/Slavn%C3%ADk "Slavník"). Litomyšl was originally a protective fortified settlement of the [Slavník dynasty](/wiki/Slavn%C3%ADk_dynasty "Slavník dynasty") principality on a significant trade route from [Bohemia](/wiki/Bohemia "Bohemia") to [Moravia](/wiki/Moravia "Moravia"). In 1259, Litomyšl was promoted to a town by King [Ottokar II](/wiki/Ottokar_II_of_Bohemia "Ottokar II of Bohemia").[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

During the following centuries the town was owned by various noble families: Kostka of Postupice, [Pernštejn](/wiki/Pern%C5%A1tejn_family "Pernštejn family"), Trauttmansdorff, Waldstein-Wartemberg and last [Thurn und Taxis](/wiki/House_of_Thurn_und_Taxis "House of Thurn und Taxis"). The Litomyšl Castle was built in 1568–1581 by the Pernštejns.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

From 30 April 1344 till its suppression in 1474 the town was the seat of the Latin Catholic [Diocese of Litomyšl](/wiki/Diocese_of_Litomy%C5%A1l "Diocese of Litomyšl") until its territory was merged back into the (meanwhile Metropolitan Arch) [Diocese of Prague](/wiki/Archdiocese_of_Prague "Archdiocese of Prague"). In 1970 it was nominally restored as [titular see](/wiki/Titular_see "Titular see").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

A congregation of the proto-Protestant brotherhood [Unitas Fratrum](/wiki/Moravian_Church "Moravian Church") (Unity of the Brethren) was documented in Litomyšl as early as 1490. The Brethren flourished in Litomyšl, being protected by the Kostka family. The Brethren founded a printing press there. The town, along with [Mladá Boleslav](/wiki/Mlad%C3%A1_Boleslav "Mladá Boleslav"), became a major centre for the Brethren. However, after the Catholic emperor emerged victorious from the [Schmalkaldic War](/wiki/Schmalkaldic_War "Schmalkaldic War"), bishop [Jan Augusta](/wiki/Jan_Augusta "Jan Augusta") was captured and imprisoned in 1548 and the Brethren were expelled from Litomyšl and many emigrated to Poland and Moravia.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In the 19th century, Litomyšl ceased to be the main economical centre of the region, but remained the cultural and educational centre.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4) Until 1918, the town was a part of [Austria-Hungary](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary"), head of the district with the same name, one of the 94 *Bezirkshauptmannschaften* in Bohemia.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Existence of the Jewish community is documented at least from the late 16th century. During [the Holocaust](/wiki/The_Holocaust "The Holocaust"), in 1942, the last families were deported.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Litomyšl had a German-speaking community until it was [expelled](/wiki/Expulsion_of_Germans_from_Czechoslovakia "Expulsion of Germans from Czechoslovakia") in 1945 as a result of the [Beneš decrees](/wiki/Bene%C5%A1_decrees "Beneš decrees").

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Transport")]

The I/35 road (the section from [Hradec Králové](/wiki/Hradec_Kr%C3%A1lov%C3%A9 "Hradec Králové") to [Svitavy](/wiki/Svitavy "Svitavy"), part of the [European route E442](/wiki/European_route_E442 "European route E442")) passes through the town.

Litomyšl is the starting point of the railway line of local importance heading to [Choceň](/wiki/Choce%C5%88 "Choceň"). The town is served by three train stations and stops.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Culture")]

Since 1946, the town hosts *Smetanova Litomyšl*, a large annual festival of classical music. It bears the name of the composer [Bedřich Smetana](/wiki/Bed%C5%99ich_Smetana "Bedřich Smetana"), who is the most famous local native.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

There is an extensive permanent exhibition of [Olbram Zoubek](/wiki/Olbram_Zoubek "Olbram Zoubek")'s sculptures and art in Litomyšl Castle Vault Gallery.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

### Language

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Language")]

Until the late 19th century, the Litomyšl area had its own unique [variety](/wiki/Variety_(linguistics) "Variety (linguistics)") of the [Czech language](/wiki/Czech_language "Czech language"). This variety, named *Teták* by linguist Henning Andersen (after its word for "five", /tet/, as opposed to Standard Czech *pět* /pjet/), underwent an unusual [sound change](/wiki/Sound_change "Sound change"): [bilabial consonants](/wiki/Bilabial_consonants "Bilabial consonants") (/p/, /b/, /m/) became [alveolars](/wiki/Alveolar_consonant "Alveolar consonant") (/t/, /d/, /n/) before [front vowels](/wiki/Front_vowel "Front vowel"). Examples are /tekɲe/ ('nicely'; Standard Czech *pěkně*), /diːlej/ ('white'; Standard Czech *bílý*) and /nesto/ ('town'; Standard Czech *město*).[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Sights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/M%C4%9Bstsk%C3%BD_d%C5%AFm_U_ryt%C3%AD%C5%99%C5%AF_%28Litomy%C5%A1l%29%2C_Smetanovo_n%C3%A1m._110.JPG/250px-M%C4%9Bstsk%C3%BD_d%C5%AFm_U_ryt%C3%AD%C5%99%C5%AF_%28Litomy%C5%A1l%29%2C_Smetanovo_n%C3%A1m._110.JPG)](/wiki/File:M%C4%9Bstsk%C3%BD_d%C5%AFm_U_ryt%C3%AD%C5%99%C5%AF_(Litomy%C5%A1l),_Smetanovo_n%C3%A1m._110.JPG)

House *U Rytířů*

The main landmark of Litomyšl is the [Litomyšl Castle](/wiki/Litomy%C5%A1l_Castle "Litomyšl Castle"), one of the largest Renaissance castles. The buildings of the castle precincts are exceptional for their architectural [refinement](/wiki/Refinement_(culture) "Refinement (culture)"). The castle complex also includes the birthplace of Bedřich Smetana, carriage house, stables, riding school, castle brewery, castle park and French-style garden. The castle complex was added to the [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") [World Heritage List](/wiki/World_Heritage_List "World Heritage List") in 1999.[[15]](#cite_note-sights-15)

Next to the castle is the Piarist college with the seat of members of the order, the Church of the Finding the Holy Cross and adjacent monastery gardens. Today the gardens serve as a town park. The gardens include sculptures by [Olbram Zoubek](/wiki/Olbram_Zoubek "Olbram Zoubek").[[15]](#cite_note-sights-15)

On the elongated square, which is almost 500 metres (1,600 ft) long and one of the largest in [Central Europe](/wiki/Central_Europe "Central Europe"), is a town hall of [Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_architecture "Gothic architecture") origin and series of Renaissance and Baroque houses, many with arcades and vaulted ground floor rooms. One of the most significant burgher houses is the house *U Rytířů* ("At the Knights"), a Renaissance house built from 1540 to 1546 with a notable stone façade.[[15]](#cite_note-sights-15)

Litomyšl is also home to the "Portmoneum", a museum of the artist and writer [Josef Váchal](/wiki/Josef_V%C3%A1chal "Josef Váchal") in the home of his admirer Josef Portman, who commissioned Váchal's murals and painted furniture in the house.[[15]](#cite_note-sights-15)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Litomy%C5%A1l&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Notable people")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Litomysl_%2826840088839%29.jpg/250px-Litomysl_%2826840088839%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Litomysl_(26840088839).jpg)

Statue of [Bedřich Smetana](/wiki/Bed%C5%99ich_Smetana "Bedřich Smetana") on the Smetanovo Square

* [Jan Augusta](/wiki/Jan_Augusta "Jan Augusta") (1500–1572), bishop of the Unitas Fratrum and Protestant reformer; lived here
* [Magdalena Dobromila Rettigová](/wiki/Magdalena_Dobromila_Rettigov%C3%A1 "Magdalena Dobromila Rettigová") (1785–1845), author of the first cookbook written in Czech; lived here in 1834–1845
* [August von Jilek](/wiki/August_von_Jilek "August von Jilek") (1819–1898) physician and oceanographer
* [Bedřich Smetana](/wiki/Bed%C5%99ich_Smetana "Bedřich Smetana") (1824–1884), classical composer
* [Julius Mařák](/wiki/Julius_Ma%C5%99%C3%A1k "Julius Mařák") (1832–1899), painter and graphic designer
* [Eduard Pospichal](/wiki/Eduard_Pospichal "Eduard Pospichal") (1838–1905), Austrian botanist
* [Josef Kořenský](/wiki/Josef_Ko%C5%99ensk%C3%BD "Josef Kořenský") (1847–1938), traveller, educator and writer; worked here as a teacher in 1871–1874
* [Zdeněk Nejedlý](/wiki/Zden%C4%9Bk_Nejedl%C3%BD "Zdeněk Nejedlý") (1878–1962), musicologist and politician
* [Božena Jelínková-Jirásková](/wiki/Bo%C5%BEena_Jel%C3%ADnkov%C3%A1-Jir%C3%A1skov%C3%A1 "Božena Jelínková-Jirásková") (1880–1951), painter
* [Arne Novák](/wiki/Arne_Nov%C3%A1k "Arne Novák") (1880–1939), critic and historian of literature
* [Zdeněk Kopal](/wiki/Zden%C4%9Bk_Kopal "Zdeněk Kopal") (1914–1993), astronomer
* [Karel Píč](/wiki/Karel_P%C3%AD%C4%8D "Karel Píč") (1920–1995), Esperantist and writer
* [Miroslav Macek](/wiki/Miroslav_Macek "Miroslav Macek") (1944–2024), politician
* [Václav Jílek](/wiki/V%C3%A1clav_J%C3%ADlek "Václav Jílek") (born 1976), football manager
* [Markéta Adamová](/wiki/Mark%C3%A9ta_Adamov%C3%A1 "Markéta Adamová") (born 1984), politician
